query: We have 18 project skills in .agents/skills now and most are one-off maintenance procedures. Design a skill library so only one dispatcher is normally visible but I can still invoke each runbook explicitly.
Can you consolidate add-locale, dependency-update, and release-recovery into a runbooks skill? First check whether they actually belong together and keep the inner procedures available on demand.
our skill list is getting noisy. i want a skill-of-skills for rare ops tasks, with some kind of catalog so the agent can find the right nested workflow
Create an operations-handler-library from analyze-alert and create-support-email-reply. Preserve their scripts and make sure nested SKILL.md files do not get registered separately.
We crossed ten active skills and performance is getting worse. Group the manually invoked ones behind a dispatcher and audit Codex and Claude skill discovery paths before cutover.
I have two emergency-only Agent Skills attached: rotate-credentials and recover-backup. I did not choose a name. Build the appropriate library and validate both entries.
Make a project-level meta-skill that routes `$runbooks add-locale` to the complete add-locale skill without loading dependency maintenance instructions too.
Please move our exceptional Flathub repair and stale-PR integration skills out of the default skill catalog, but do not make them unreachable or duplicate their registrations.
Review these five skills and decide which should remain standalone versus live in one or more coherent libraries. Do not create a miscellaneous hidden-skills bucket.
Add a new entry to our existing runbooks-library, update its Available Skills table, and verify the sync script will not recreate the old standalone skill.
Create a new skill that teaches agents how to rotate service credentials safely. It will be installed as a normal standalone skill.
Generalize this Mini Diarium add-locale skill so I can reuse it in unrelated repositories.
Install the spreadsheet and PDF skills from our curated marketplace into my user-level Codex setup.
List all Agent Skills currently installed in this repository and show their descriptions.
Our agent client needs recursive discovery of .agents/skills and an activate_skill API. Implement the client-side scanner and prompt catalog.
Improve the trigger description of the existing todo-manager skill and benchmark whether it activates correctly.
Write an incident-response runbook in docs/operations.md for engineers to follow during outages. This is documentation, not an Agent Skill.
Create a TypeScript library that exports our shared frontend validation helpers.
Package these skills as a Codex plugin with a manifest and marketplace entry.
Update the implementation instructions inside dependency-update; its lockfile validation step is wrong.
